title: Ostende
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Hupsa! jonker Jan,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Die wel ruiter worden kan.ן¿½


ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boon tidings to der Breitmann came
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ash he at table end,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dereן¿½s right goot fisch at Blankenberghe,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und oysters in Ostend.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Denn to Ostland ve will reiten gaen,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To Ostland oן¿½er de sand,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dou und I mit pridle drawn
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For dere ish de oyster land.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und vhen dey shtood bei Ostersee,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere de waters roar like sin,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere coom five hundert fischer volk
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To dake der Breitmann in.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Gotts doonder! Should ve doomple down
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Amoong de waters plue,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I kess youן¿½d vant more help from me
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dan I should vant from you!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½If you hat peen vhere I hafe peen
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und see vot I hafe see,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere de surf rise oop nine tausend feet,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In de land of Nieuw Jarsie
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und schwimmed dat surf ash I hafe schwimmed,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Peside de Jersey stranן¿½ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ From dat day fortן¿½ de Ostland men
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Shdeered glear of der Breitemann.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot von ding set him schvearin so,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I dinked heן¿½d nefer cease,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De Ostend oysters kostet more
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In Ostend als Paris.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hans asked an anciendt fisherman,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To ן¿½splain dis if he may,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und says he, ן¿½Mijn Heer deyן¿½re beter hier
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Als ein hundert leagues afay.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Und as de oysters beter hier
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of course dey kostet moreן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Breitmann dook his bilcrim shdaff,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und toorned him to de toor.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Says Hans, ן¿½De Vlaemsche fischermen
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Can sheat de vorldt I pet
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey sheaten von anoder too,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Allן¿½s fisch to a Dutchmanן¿½s net.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Der king peginned a palace hier,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De palace hat to shtop,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He foundt de beoples sheaten so
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He gife de bildin oop.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Aldough das Leben hier ish goot,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ad least Ostend-siblyן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So shpoke der Breitemann und cut
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat city py de sea.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles G. Leland represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Charles G. Leland's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
